Task: Real-time WebSocket Streaming for AI Analysis
Description: Stream AI analysis results (features, action items) to frontend in real-time via WebSocket instead of polling. Reduces perceived latency and improves UX.
Add support for multilingual meeting transcripts (Vietnamese, Japanese, Korean, Chinese). Integrate language detection in transcript_parsers.py and adapt AI prompts per language.
Compare AI outputs across multiple meetings (same project/team). Track feature evolution, recurring action items, and decision patterns over time.
Detect and merge duplicate/similar features extracted from different meetings using semantic similarity. Extend FeatureMatching schema.
Allow users to create/save custom prompt templates for different meeting types (standup, retro, planning, design review). Store in DB per user/org.
Support uploading multiple meeting files at once. Queue them for sequential processing with a unified progress dashboard.
Aggregate analytics across all meetings: total features extracted, action item completion rates, meeting frequency, cost per meeting, speaker participation trends.
Track action items across meetings. Auto-detect completion status in subsequent meetings. Send email reminders for overdue items.
Calculate a composite score per meeting based on: action items generated, decisions made, participant engagement, duration efficiency.
Dashboard showing AI cost per meeting/user/team. Set monthly cost budgets with alerts. Extend existing cost_tracker.py.
Multi-tenant organization support: invite team members, shared meeting library, org-level settings, role-based access (admin/member/viewer).
Real-time collaborative editing of transcripts (like Google Docs). Multiple users can edit speaker names, fix text, and add annotations simultaneously.
Allow team members to comment on extracted features, vote on priority, and add context. Thread-based discussions on each feature.
Generate shareable links for analysis results (read-only). Support password protection and expiry dates.
Export extracted features directly as Jira issues or Linear tickets. Map priority, assignee, effort estimates to issue fields.
Send meeting summaries, action items, and feature lists to Slack/Teams channels after analysis completes.
Connect to Google Meet or Zoom API to auto-import meeting recordings. Trigger analysis pipeline automatically.
Link meetings to calendar events. Auto-tag with attendees, meeting title, and recurring meeting context.
Allow users to configure webhooks triggered on: analysis complete, action item created, evaluation score below threshold.
Searchable meeting archive with filters by date, participants, meeting type, tags. Full-text search across transcripts and features.
Drag-and-drop Kanban board to manage extracted action items (Todo → In Progress → Done). Per-meeting and cross-meeting views.
Auto-generate a visual roadmap from extracted features across meetings. Timeline view grouped by module/phase/priority.
Allow users to record meetings directly in the browser. Upload recording to pipeline without external tools.
Full dark mode support. Allow users to customize accent colors. Persist preference per user.
Automated testing, linting, type-checking, and deployment on push. Separate staging/production environments.
Scale arq workers independently. Add Kubernetes manifests or Cloud Run config for auto-scaling based on queue depth.
Log all user actions (login, upload, edit, export, delete) with timestamps and IP. Admin-viewable audit trail.
Add social login via Google and Microsoft OAuth2. Map to existing user accounts or auto-create.
Improve existing semantic cache with vector similarity search. Cache at chunk level instead of full transcript. Reduce repeat analysis costs by ~60%.
Auto-generate professional PDF meeting minutes with branding: summary, features table, action items, speaker stats. Download or email.
Analyze sentiment trends per speaker throughout the meeting. Visualize positive/negative/neutral shifts on a timeline.
AI identifies risks, blockers, and dependencies mentioned in meetings. Link to specific features and action items.
Run two prompt versions on the same transcript simultaneously. Compare extraction quality via evaluation metrics. Auto-select winner.
In-app notification center: analysis complete, action item reminders, team mentions, evaluation alerts.
